steps.title: Use the rectangle area formula
steps.content: For any rectangle, area = width × length. The problem gives area $A = w(w+79)$ and tells us width $= w$.

steps.title: Solve for length
steps.content: Divide both sides of $w \cdot \text{length} = w(w+79)$ by $w$: $$\text{length} = \frac{w(w+79)}{w} = (w+79)$$

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A) $79$ — just a constant, not the full length factor.
  • B) $w(w+79)$ — this is the entire area, not the length.
  • C) $(w+79)$ — matches the derived length.
  • D) $w$ — this is the width, not the length.
That confirms C is correct.
